title: Oritrophium cocuyense
taxonomy: Oritrophium cocuyense is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to South America. The classification of Oritrophium cocuyense is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Asterales, the family Asteraceae, the genus Oritrophium, and the species O. cocuyense. Oritrophium cocuyense is a part of the subfamily Carduoideae. The classification of Oritrophium cocuyense is still a subject of ongoing research and may be subject to revision.

intro: Oritrophium cocuyense, also known as Cocuy's crown of thorns, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing symptoms of pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Oritrophium cocuyense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
